{{Quote|Mesa must rebuild Otoh Sancture in memory of all the brave Gungans wesa lost.|Boss Gallo|<ref name="gb" />}}
During a war between the [[Gungan/Legends|Gungan]] tribes on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]] in [[3000 BBY]], a '''battle''' was fought on the ruins of the [[City/Legends|city]] [[Otoh Sancture]] between the [[Militiagung/Legends|Militiagungs]] under the command of [[Boss/Legends|Boss]] [[Gallo/Legends|Gallo]] and [[Bursa/Legends|bursas]] under the influence of Boss [[Rogoe/Legends|Rogoe]]. Gallo had previously refused to take sides in the long-raging war, leading Rogoe, the war's secret mastermind, to order the [[Destruction of Otoh Sancture|destruction]] of Gallo's home Otoh Sancture.

Gallo escaped the destruction and vowed to avenge his home while rebuilding it. He assembled an [[Army/Legends|army]] in a nearby [[Forest/Legends|forest]] with which he [[Death/Legends|slaughtered]] the bursas and destroyed their nests. In the aftermath of the battle, Gallo united with the rogue [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Marsune (Gungan)|Marsune]], and together the pair united the other [[Gungan/Legends|Gungan]] tribes against Rogoe, who was eventually killed at the [[Raid on Spearhead]].

==Prelude==
===The destruction of Otoh Sancture===
{{Quote|No! Mesa People are bein destroyen!|Boss Gallo|<ref name="gb" />}}
{{Main|Destruction of Otoh Sancture}}
During the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo's]] [[War of the Gungan tribes|war]] between the [[Gungan/Legends|Gungan]] tribes in [[3000 BBY]], [[Boss/Legends|Boss]] [[Gallo/Legends|Gallo]] of the [[City/Legends|city]] [[Otoh Sancture]] was the only boss to remain neutral, as he refused to take up arms against his fellow Gungangs. Gallo's power was considerable, and Boss [[Rogoe/Legends|Rogoe]] of [[Spearhead/Legends|Spearhead]], the mastermind behind the war who was believed to be [[Death/Legends|dead]] or a myth by the rest of the Gungan community, decided that it would be prudent to eliminate Gallo before he had the chance to align himself, and so he ordered the death of his fellow boss.<ref name="gb" />

[[File:SantctureMassacre.jpg|thumb|right|280px|The destruction of Otoh Sancture.]]
On the day of Otoh Sancture's [[Sacred Feast]], Rogoe had his [[Militiagung/Legends|militiagungs]] dispatch wild [[Bursa/Legends|bursas]] to attack the defenseless city. As per tradition, Gallo had ventured forth from the settlement to gather food for the Sacred Feast, and as a result, he was not present when the bursas attacked. The creatures left no building standing nor any civilian alive in their wake, turning Otoh Sancture into a desolate wasteland.<ref name="gb" />

===Gallo's flight===
{{Quote|Hesa sent thosa Bursas to pound yousa for good.|One of Boss Rogoe's minions telling Gallo that Rogoe had ordered the massacre|<ref name="gb" />}}
[[File:MarsuneGalloGlurrgs.jpg|thumb|left|260px|Gallo meets with Marsune]]
Gallo saw the destruction and fled north into the forest atop his [[Kaadu/Legends|kaadu]] with the five [[Nerf/Legends|nerfs]] he had gathered for the feast in tow. On his journey, he came across the [[Marsune's base|base]] of the rogue [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Marsune (Gungan)|Marsune]], and his [[Marsune's gang|gang]]. Gallo requested aid, but the captain refused and laughed at him, although he did exchange four worker [[glurrg]]s in exchange for the nerfs Gallo had found. Continuing through the swamp with his new acquisitions, Gallo spotted several militiagungs from Spearhead shepherding wild bursas and confronted them, leading [[Unidentified militiagung (Otoh Sancture)|one]] of them to reveal that Rogoe was responsible.<ref name="gb" />

Outraged, Gallo resolved to wreak revenge on Rogoe for the massacre and told the militiagungs of his intent before continuing on with his glurrgs.<ref name="gb" /> Coming to a clearing that boasted resources such as [[Nova crystal/Legends|nova crystals]] and [[Ore/Legends|ore]],<ref name="Prima" /> Gallo decided that he would rebuild Otoh Sancture in memory of his dead people.<ref name="gb" /> He began with a [[command center]], with which he could summon more glurrgs who could build [[prefab shelter]]s to support an [[Army/Legends|army]] of Gungan recruits with which to retake Otoh Sancture's ruins.<ref name="Prima" />

==The battle==
[[File:SanctureBattle.jpg|thumb|right|200px|The Gungans fight the bursas at the entrance to Otoh Sancture]]
With ten militiagungs and twenty kaadu mounted troopers,<ref name="Prima" /> Gallo crossed a river into Otoh Sancture. They encountered several bursas, which they fought and killed. The path to the ruins of Otoh Sancture was blocked by several of the bursas' nests, which Gallo and his soldiers razed. With the entrance breached, they fought the remaining bursas in Otoh Sancture and destroyed all of their nests.<ref name="gb" />

==Aftermath==
{{Quote|Mesa thought about yousa words. Ugh—mesa have no stomach for this Rogoe. Mesa wish to see himsa fall. Mesa will help yousa in yousa grand quest, Gallo.|Marsune to Gallo|<ref name="gb" />}}
Marsune arrived at Otoh Sancture and revealed that he had a change of heart and wished to join Gallo's quest to defeat Rogoe. Working together, Gallo and Marsune were able to [[Battle of the Forest Cities|unite]] the other Gungan tribes against Rogoe, including [[Otoh Jahai]], [[Otoh Langua/Legends|Otoh Langua]], [[Otoh Raban]], and [[Otoh Urs]]. The united Bosses killed Rogoe at the [[Raid on Spearhead]], and Gallo founded a new [[Capital/Legends|capital]] called [[Otoh Gunga/Legends|Otoh Gunga]] on the ruins of Spearhead. Some three thousand years later, the events of the war were chronicled by Boss [[Rugor Nass/Legends|Rugor Nass]], a distant descendant of Gallo's.<ref name="gb" />

==Behind the scenes==
The battle of Otoh Sancture was first mentioned in ''[[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds: Prima's Official Strategy Guide]]'' by [[Steven L. Kent]],<ref name="Prima">[[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds: Prima's Official Strategy Guide|''Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds'': Prima's Official Strategy Guide]]</ref>—released on [[November 7]], [[2001]]<ref name="PrimaDate">{{Amazon|url=Star-Wars-Galactic-Battlegrounds-Official/dp/0761537503|text=Star Wars Galactic Battlegrounds: Prima's Official Strategy Guide}}</ref>—before appearing in the [[LucasArts]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds]]'',<ref name="gb" /> released on [[November 11]], 2001.<ref name="GBDate">{{GameSpot|url=games/star-wars-galactic-battlegrounds|text=Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds}}</ref> In the game, it appears in the first level of the Gungan campaign; "Otoh Sancture," where the player controls Gallo and carries out the attack on the bursas.<ref name="gb" />
